quality.rationale: 区分同时性测量与光行差视觉效应，需多步结合洛伦兹变换与光速传播时间推导

origin.question: Problem 1.5. A distant camera snaps a photograph of a speeding bullet (velocity v) with length b in its rest frame. Behind the bullet and parallel to its path is a meter stick, at rest with respect to the camera. The direction to the camera is an angle $\alpha$ from the direction of the bullet's velocity. What will be the apparent length of the bullet as seen in the photo? (i.e. How much of the meter stick is hidden?).
origin.solution: Solution 1.5. The usual $\mathsf{b} / \gamma$ answer refers to a measurement made simultaneously in the lab frame, but the photons are not emitted simultaneously in the lab frame. They are received simultaneously so that photon 2 (see diagram) has to travel for an extra time $\mathsf{b}' \cos \alpha$ , where $\mathsf{b}'$ is the apparent length. From the Lorentz transformation$$\mathbf {b} = \Delta \mathbf {x} = \gamma (\Delta \mathbf {x}^{\prime} - \beta \Delta \mathbf {t}^{\prime}) = \gamma (\mathbf {b}^{\prime} - \beta \mathbf {b}^{\prime} \cos \alpha),$$so that$$b^{\prime} = b / \gamma (1 - \beta \cos \alpha).$$![](images/0b5058323b363dc15183a42e2d8ee51c64512eaba814d09599d9511847030c37.jpg)
